rule: Blank values are missing, never zero.
A zero requires explicit outcome assessment and a source-supported denominator.
Primary risk unit is participants with >=1 event; event counts are supplementary.
Randomized, dosed, safety, and observed-at-window denominators are stored separately.
Product A and Product B are never pooled together.
MDD and TRD are never silently pooled.
Single and repeated administrations are separate strata.
Niacin, inactive placebo, minimal-dose psilocybin, and active treatment are distinct comparator classes.
Exact and overlapping windows are separate; onset and resolution prevent double-counting.
Solicited, structured, spontaneous, and unclear harm collection are compatibility variables.
No arbitrary continuity correction is permitted.
If all compatible studies have zero events, report numerator/denominator and exact bound; do not estimate heterogeneity.
A statistician freezes model choice before independent extraction results are unmasked.
Reclassify one and two possible missed events; leave one study out; vary attribution and ascertainment assumptions.
No inference to recreational use, natural mushrooms, unscreened populations, or unmonitored settings.
GRADE is outcome-, comparator-, window-, and stratum-specific.
Not reported and assessed-zero are displayed separately in every table.
Every published estimate must resolve to machine-readable source rows and executable code.
